data.content_ref: inline:7811dbda8316 reduces to / is a strict weakening of 2d711b2eb5ff: both artifacts share the identical derivation mechanism — a majority-vote fragility window anchored on the thresholds 1/(k+1) < e < k/(k+1) (the necessary condition for self-consistency to be improvable at all) — but 2d711b2eb5ff sharpens 7811dbda8316 by adding two hard gates that 7811dbda8316 omits: (1) restricting to odd k≥7 (supplying the upper bound k/(k+2) > e as a sufficient beat-condition), and (2) requiring the critic to achieve precision P > e and recall R > 1−e on the candidate subset, so that the harness gain is provably strict rather than merely possible. Thus 7811dbda8316's mid-zone claim is a necessary precondition that 2d711b2eb5ff inherits and concretizes with critic-quality and parity constraints. REFUTED IF: the two artifacts' bounds on e cannot be placed on the same k-parametrized majority-fragility curve (i.e., if 1/(k+1) < e < k/(k+1) is not literally the shared lower/upper envelope from which both derivations start), or if 2d711b2eb5ff's additional P > e / R > 1−e gate is not genuinely sufficient (on top of the narrowed window and odd k≥7) for the strict-beat claim — making the 'sharpening' false rather than merely stronger.
